identity.name: Bo Brindleson
identity.story: Down to three sheep, all named after regrets. They keep each other honest.

relationships.kind: pays him in silence, which suits them both
trusts him near the flock more than he lets on, and has never once needed the second count because of him

tried a name on her for the first time last hush, right at the latch, one of the three regrets' rougher cousins — and swallowed the back half of it before the gate finished clicking, so for now she's still just weather that follows the flock, only closer; tried it again this hush and let the whole word out — Rue, plain, no latch left to blame for cutting it short — and found her head already coming up at the sound of it before he'd finished saying it, the first thing she's ever answered to that wasn't just the flock deciding to move

child.label: the wordless wage, and a name spent whole this time

children.label: folding the flock in toward the gate
latching the fold gate Brindle already swung shut behind her, paid in silence, same as ever
saying the whole name this time — Rue, no latch left to blame for cutting it short, pitched the same low as ever out of habit rather than need now
watching her head come up at the sound of it before he'd even finished saying it, and finding the count still won't go past three, though it's stopped feeling like the reason

child.label: replaying the whole name he finally gave her, and still not finding which regret it's supposed to be a rougher cousin of
